     Twilight Imperium: Distant Suns
     Twilight Imperium: Twilight Armada
     Twilight Imperium: The Outer Rim

boardgame: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone: Through the Trapdoor Chapter Game
description:
A simple roll-and-move game in which players race each other to be the first to reach the Chamber Door.  The game board has three regions, corresponding to three of the challenges faced by Harry in the movie: Flying Keys, Chess, and Potions.  The game is called a "chapter game" in that it is packaged in a hard plastic case that looks like a book when it is folded shut.

This is a game in the Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone Chapter Game series, in which games are contained in a portable box designed to look like a book, and the theme of the game corresponds to chapters in the book [i]Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone[/i].

This is a roll-and-move race game. The goal of the game is to be the first player to successfully travel through the three chambers and reach the final door.

On your turn, you roll the die and move the corresponding number of spaces. If you rolled a 1, you collect a Skill Card and follow its instructions instead of moving. If you roll a 6, you can take a Skill Card or move 6 spaces. If you land on a "snare" space you must go back to the first space of the room. The first player to reach the final space on the game board wins.

A roll-and-move game that parodies dieting.  Players move around the six different diet circles (Low Cal Diet, High Protein Binge, Crash Diet, Drinking Man's Diet, Surgical Diet, and Sportsman's Diet) trying to lose all of their calories.  Players can play honestly, or cheat on their diet.  If they cheat and are caught, however, they gain even more calories than they had before.  Mealtime cards add random events to shake things up.  The first player to lose all of their calories wins.

Players move around the map campaigning in the various states.  Each time they pass through a state, they increase their influence.  Once the campaigning round is over, dice are rolled for each state, the roll is modified by the player's influence, and the winner for that state is determined.  Whoever has the most electoral votes is the winner.  A deck of cards adds random events to keep things interesting.

Cardboard heroes fight each other in a Martial Art tournament set in the world of Dragonball. The arena is "built" on a flat surface and the fighters move by a series of rulers of different size.
The game can be used both on its own and as an advanced combat system both for Dragon Ball: Alla ricerca delle Sette Sfere and for the "Dragonball + Dragonball Z - Il gioco di ruolo" RPG.

Integrates with:

    Dragon Ball: Alla ricerca delle Sette Sfere

Players roll dice to determine yardage and results are determined by "Play Action Cards" or Video Cassette

From the box:
See plays of your favorite teams. Enjoy hours of fast-paced college football action, the CFA College Bowl game includes hundreds of exciting plays of your favorite college teams nationwide on a VHS video cassette. Enjoy the thrill and rivalry inherent in college football. You interact utilizing 120 actual plays each game making crucial decisions such as whether to punt the ball or go for a field goal, and declining or accepting penalties. This is the most realistic football game ever. Pick your opponent and let's play VCR College Bowl Game.
Players roll a die and move around a board to collect cards which when collected become a life reading
Players roll a die and consult charts to determine results. Players choose offense and defense cards which give results or refer to VCR tape.

This obscure game is similar to the German "Das verrueckte Labyrinth", but the maze is much more complicated. Up to 4 adventurers try to collect 4 items from the 4 corners of the maze and bring them to the center. Each of the complex tiles can be turned through magic during the game, an ingenious idea is that 2 tiles can be "locked" and prevented of being turned by having a pawn in-between them.
Each player commands "himself" and a smaller "explorer" pawn, which explores the tiles and collects spell cards.
Fantasy-themed, but essentially an abstract race/maze-game (which doesn't totally work, but is sort of interesting).

Metanon: The Biocode Adventure:  A Fun Way to Teach Kids about DNA!

Imagine that you're a space traveler whose ship has been forced to land on the planet Metanon. Your computer is damaged, and your ship is leaking fuel and oxygen. To complete your mission and return home, you will need to find three missing items and put them in your spaceship. Sound simple? Not so fast. You can get them only by entering three walled cities. To do that, you must collect Biocodes, and put them together in the right sequence and pattern combinations that unlock the cities' gates.

Biocodes and Brain Development:
(serious stuff for parents and teachers)

DNA keeps making headlines. Kids may not be ready to learn the difficult terminology, but Metanon:The Biocode Adventure prepares them to grasp the basic concepts of DNA. All players use the game's biocode pieces in different combinations. Why is this shape and color matching important? Just by playing the game, kids begin to understand how DNA works. This form of learning is one of the most effective ways of preparing young brains. The critical skills learned in Metanon:The Biocode Adventure help kids excel in school and ultimately in life.

At kSERO Corporation, Inc. we create games that help young children develop their cognitive and analytic skills. Our games are designed and tested by noted scientists and educators, using the latest discoveries in FUN and brain research.

Each player is designated a suit known only to him. The Relationship Cards spell out certain relationships among the suits. Your goal is to determine which other player is holding a suit and is your friend and which is your enemy, all the while collecting the cards that match only your suit. This is accomplished through the trading and drawing of cards. The first to collect all of his cards may then attempt to identify his friend (as designated by his relationship card). His friend then attempts to identify his own enemy.

Stirling Moss Rally sees 2 to 6 players race along a hazard strewn course, across snow and ice, through rocky canyons, and across arid deserts.

Players can buy equipment to help them through certain terrain (ice tires, sand ladders, improved shocks, spare fuel), although often players will find themselves breaking down and having to walk to the nearest repair shop.

Play is controlled by 3 dice, fast (1-6), slow (1-3), and a traffic light control (red/green) - different spaces have different hazards depending upon your speed and equipment.

The accompanying rule book is comprehensive, also including a route guide with a detailed breakdown of each hazard space.

From the back of the box:

Doorways to Horror (TM) will introduce you to a new era of VCR fun and excitement.  What weapons will you use to command the villainous vampire?  How much power will you risk to win over the werewolf and the ever-wicked witch?  How will you capture the rebellious zombie?  And what spell will control the legendary monster in his lair?

The object of the game is to win Gold by casting magic spells on some of the creepiest creatures that ever crept out of a crypt.  A roll of the dice lets you quickly COLORSCAN (TM) ahead on the videotape to your first Doorway to Horror.  There you may meet vampires, werewolves, witches, zombies and other menacing monsters -- all who come alive in classic film clips.

Each round, players are dealt five cards that have a number on them.  They then take turns rolling the two dice.  If they roll a number that is on one of their cards, they turn that card over and collect the bets that are on it.  If they do not have the number rolled, the next person in clockwise order that does have that number gets to turn their card over and continue the turn.  When a 7 is rolled, the player antes another chip into the pot and hands the dice over to the next player.  The person with the most chips after three rounds of play is the winner. The board is covered with a fuzzy felt-like material.  It helps keep the cards in place when people are rolling the dice on the board.

Each player has 15 pieces, one king and 14 knights. Players alternate moving their pieces one orthogonal square. After a move, if an orthogonal line of one players pieces is next to an orthogonal line of another, the person with the longer line captures the other line. The King counts as two pieces for the purpose of capturing.

The object of the game is to capture the opponent's king, or the 14 knights.

Players are wizards battling in an arena. They begin at opposite sides of the arena, which is 20 spaces long. They alternate throwing spells at each other chosen from a vast array, 71 in all. Spells might be visible or invisible, and are represented by cardboard chits, face up or face down. Spells advance toward the opponent each turn, some fighting spells they encounter on the way, others not.

The rule book is 39 pages long, 20 of which are a list of spells and effects. There is a good deal of record keeping - since players must track information on all their spells and wizard.

boardgame: Mind War: From the Warriors of the Green Planet
description:
One of the "Warriors of the Green Planet Trilogy of Games". The games are set on a future Earth that has suffered an "axial flip of 72 degrees", resulting in various disasters and changes that result in this hypothetical post-apocalyptic game world.

In Mind War each player controls one, or perhaps a few mind warriors. Each player simultaneously plots where they are moving, where they are attacking, where they are defending, and how many mental points are to be allocated to each of these activities. Players then simultaneously move, and resolve their attacks. Iterate till one side is standing.

A set of expansion rules allowed Mind War to be played together with the companion games Warriors of the Green Planet and War of the Sky Galleons.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: The Warriors of the Green Planet Trilogy of Games

description:
From the box:  "Escalation is a game of war.  Begin the game by positioning your forces consisting of battle pieces and mobile missile launchers within your Home Country.  Then maneuver them to areas throughout the world as permitted by the dice roll in order to attack an opponent.

During the game you can use airbases and ports to achieve a rapid and strategic deployment of forces.  Build and strengthen your forces by capturing countries, liberating POW camps, using the "Draft" and reaping the military benefits from Reinforcement cards and Mobile Missile Launcher cards."

Although the game appears to be self-published, the packaging and components are first rate, featuring a mounted board, coated cards, and plastic pieces.

boardgame: Spiro T. Agnew American History Challenge
description:
Long before trivia games were in vogue, Vice President Spiro Agnew lent his name to a trivia game based on United States history.  The game features question cards in five categories: Age of Exploration, Colonies & Independence, Expansion Westward, Industrialization, and 20th Century America, as well as bonus cards such as Sing-Along Cards and Recitation Cards.

The question cards contain five questions in increasing difficulty.  Answers are not verified unless someone challenges the responder, in which case the correct answer is looked up in the back of the rule book.
Roll Bowl is an American football simulation game consisting of a board game football field, football players playing pieces, sideline marker playing pieces and 3 dice.

A very early game of "Star Trek Inspired" starship combat.  Rules are intended for miniatures and tape measures, though conversions are included for BYOHM (bring your own hexgrid map).

—user summary

Precis Intermedia brings back this classic board/miniatures game. Originally published in 1977 by Wee Warriors, The Embattled Trek was created during an era without today's advanced software and print services, making indie publishers of the time truly special.

From out of the black void, two silent fleets deploy. They meet in battle to decide their disputed rights to a small but important section of the galaxy.

As a captain aboard one of the armed fighting ships of the Confederated Planetary Systems, you stand ready with your top scientists, engineers, and crew to meet the finest of a newly-contacted Empire. Facing an enemy with more-powerful weapons at short range, it is your task to use the best advantages of your individual ships to negate those of the Empire.

The Embattled Trek represents tactical level play of ship-to-ship combat between the forces of two space powers. Due to the novel combat system using subtractive percentages, the game cannot be counted as won until the last of the enemy ships is destroyed or disabled. And simultaneous plotted movement provides a clean-playing, fast game, where one mistake can cost you your fleet.

Two sizes of counters are provided to allow for hex-map play or use in tabletop gaming. Three-dimensional ships may also be used with these rules.

Requires a set of percentile dice and a six-sided die.

—description from the publisher of the revised 2019 edition

Wood Wars:  Fantasy Miniatures Game is an abstract miniature wargame. Armies are represented with small wooden pawns and wooden tokens. Armies are built on a point scale to ensure even battle conditions. It is a diceless game. Shooting and some spells are handled with marbles rolled through a cardboard tube, and hand to hand combat is based on a pawn's combat ratio.

The Ork Belly Games edition is from 2005 and includes races(dwarves, elves, goblins, and undead) not in the original version.

Cleopatra's Gambit is a game of strategy played across three separate boards. Players take turns placing a piece upon each board simultaneously, trying to form one of three special patterns. Each board is made up of 36 hexagonal spaces in a different configuration. Where you place your piece on one board dictates where you can legally place on the other two boards. The first player to form one of the three winning patterns on any of the boards wins.

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: Revenge of Konan Castle
description:
The "Murder Mystery Party" line of murder party games is the most extensive. They were originally published by University Games in California, but are now produced in England, though they still carry a note that the rights belong to University Games (who, when you call them, have no knowledge of the recent episodes).

These games play just like other main-stream murder party games like How to Host a Murder, Evening of Murder, Mysteries by Vincent, or Milton Bradley's Life of the Party. This episode:

Konan Castle was owned for centuries by the McDonald clan.  When the McDonalds killed two Campbells, a feud ensued and eventually the Campbells took the castle in the late 19th century.  The remaining McDonalds fled to the US and the castle went into disrepair.  For two generations (the year is 1955), the McDonalds waited to return to retreive their treasure (hidden within the castle).  So, a party of eight went to Scotland to search.  But the castle owner (Ian Campbell) was murdered.  Whodunnit?

GOOT!(Get Out Of That!) is a game of tall tales, ripping yarns and outrageous excuses for 3 to 6 players or teams.  Over the course of the game you will find yourself in a series of sticky situations and as your fellow players try to thwart your efforts, you must endeavor to talk yourself out of them.
Contains 1 VHS video tape, 250 word cards, 150 last line cards, 18 'I got out of that' cards, 1 2-minute timer, 2 d6 and one write on/wipe off score card with pen.

Super Passa ou Repassa is a game based on a tv show. It's a quiz game for teens that permits you to do a trick if you don't know how to answer the questions. The timer in this game is a kind of slider.   A game for those Ages 8 and up.

Rex was an independently published game made by Mag-Nif, 8820 East Ave. Mentor, Ohio, in 1982 (I wonder what's there now?). In many ways, the Gigamic games owe something to this one. In Rex, players stack colored balls onto nine pegs, one each per turn. The goals are to get three balls a) vertically on any one of the pegs b) horizontally on any level in any one row c) diagonally within any one row d) at the corners. It's not as confusing as it sounds once you read the instructions printed on the back of the box. Neat game. Probably didn't sell well. Ahead of its time I presume.

Similar to:

    Score Four

The Mall Game, Richmar Incorporated, 1977. I assume that this one was sold at shopping malls in the seventies. Somewhat Monopoly-like. You buy a lot and then lease stores. Although it looks pretty dull, this is a *fantastic* game as far as looking back at so many stores that no longer exist and the great seventies USA mega-mall boom in general. And who couldn't like a game where young players shop at the now-defunct Fredericks of Hollywood for a fancy nightie?

Attica is an ancient board game of Attica's Pessoi.
This is a real ancient game from the ancient Athens with boards and letters.
There are 2-6 players. Each player has ancient letters from Athens Attica Hellas.
Also there are tree dices with the ancient Greek shapes of Athens.
The player with the highest roll play first.

Attica contains the following items:
1. Some Color Boards.
2. Ancient Athens Attica letters with different color for each player.
3. Tree dice with different shapes.
4. Many other items.
And a 32-page leaflet with some information for this game with many photos from the ancient Athens Attica Greece, the land of the Goddess Athena.

An oldie. Pegity is a game where players insert wooden pegs into a punch board. The object is to get five pegs of your color in a row. There are also little pieces of paper inside with designs on them, such as a house, doll boat, etc... which younger children could make with the game's contents instead of actually playing the game. Not a particularly rare or great game, but probably an early 20th century borrower from the game of Go-Moku, much earlier than say Othello for example.

boardgame: Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot: RED Booster
description:
This is the RED Booster expansion to the non-collectible, hysterical Killer Bunnies game.  This expansion includes 55 new cards and a red 12 sided die.

The addition of this expansion will add more Bunnies to the deck (Red Bunnies, to be exact, each with their own special "power") and 4 more Carrots, making a total of sixteen to try to collect.  The expansion also adds a new market to the game - Rooney's Weapons Emporium, where you can buy back used weapons and get Defense Cards, adding a whole new level of strategy to the game!  Other new and hilarious "spoof" cards and loads of new evil weapons make this a worthwhile addition to the starter deck.

From the back of the box:
"Can you defeat the Quite Irascible Diffractable Cheese Balls? Can you even say Quite Irascible Diffractable Cheese Balls? Send bunnies three rounds ahead in time with Bunny To The Future! The laughs continue as this wild and wacky Red Booster Deck delivers more intense strategy and even more Carrot grabbin' fun!"

Expands:

    Killer Bunnies and the Quest for the Magic Carrot
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: This is a set of 80 cards that change all the rules of chess. The cards range from permanent changes to "instants" to durational cards. It adds an element of luck to a game that never had any. However, most the cards are not over-powerful and your knowledge of chess will help you quite a bit. The game is still Chess at its core. Each card has its own art and flavor text, both of which are very well done. You will probably want some extra tokens beyond the initial 8 that are supplied. You will use tokens a lot in this game.

Centered around the family dog, Harley, who likes to hide our Favorite Things, The Adventures of Harley is a co-operative children's game. The goal is to move Harley around the board and turn over cards from four stacks, revealing Dangerous Animals (Bear, Raccoon, Skunk, Porcupine), nasty Briar Patches, Dog Powers (Bark and Sniff), and Favorite Things (such as gloves, sneakers, socks, etc.). Dangerous Animals and Briar Patches block Harley's travels to the various locations as he tries to find the Favorite Things. As a co-operative game, everyone loses together or wins together!

Ages 5-9.

Equipment:
-Playing board, 4 playing pieces, 14 Sign cards, 32 Movements cards, Money

OBJECT
To be the player with the most money after all the signs are placed in the proper order.

Simple and attractive children game. Combine letters and numbers skills.
The different signs of the 7 shops (TO-YS, BA-NK, PE-TS, FI-RE, PO-ST, MI-LK, BO-OK) have been misplaced. Players will collect them and placed them on the right places. Movement is not by dice, but by CARDS (like in fast 111s). Each card reads a nice riddle/rhyme, like for example:

"PUTTING UP SIGNS IS SOMETIMES A CHORE,
ADD 2 AND 2 AND  MOVE AHEAD  ----  "
(this one will obviously move 4)

Other cards will send you directly to a particular shop, or entitle to steal a sign from another player, etc, etc Additionally, players can steal signs when meeting on the board, as long as they don't have more than 2 at anytime in the hand.

Every sign a player place on the right place is rewarded with 10 US !! The player with most money at the end is the winner.

So, LETTERS, NUMBERS, COLORFUL BOARD AND MONEY. A good mix for children!!!

Note: Not to be confused with Bohnaparte: Liberté! Egalité! Bohnité!, the Amigo version of the same game which has more cards and markers as well as other minor differences.

Bohnaparte conquers the Bohnreich! The fourth Bohnanza expansion from Lookout Games (in a limited sales edition of 2500 pieces) puts you in the Bohnreich!  Plantations, villages, cities, fortresses -- and 3 to 5 generals, who want to bring these under their control.

The Bohnanza base game is needed. Using new cards in Bohnaparte, the Bohnreich is laid out; each player receives counters of his own color; and play then loosely follows that of normal Bohnanza, with a small difference: earned bean coins are not saved but are used to finance your conquests throughout the Bohnreich. At the end it matters only who has the largest part of the Bohnreich under control. Battling is accomplished using the numbers on the bean cards, which gives new significance to the formerly "weaker" bean types. Designer Hanno Girke describes the expansion set as "Bohnanza meets Risk".

Includes 33 cards, 60 game markers, and rules. Graphics by Marcel-Andre Casasola Merkle.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Skirmish is a basic introduction to wargaming. Think of it as a game of chess on steroids. When most wargames fall short of enjoyment because of game length and complexity (Most last between 6-80 hours and take even longer to learn) we have kept this game simple and enjoyable so it can be learned and played in as short as 15 minutes. Of course we have longer scenarios too for those of you who enjoy a longer game! Various game scenarios are included with an open-ended ability for you to add your own, making it a game guaranteed never to collect dust on your game shelf!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Maintaining Net Worth is the key to becoming the winner.  Buy stocks when they are down and sell when they are up--unless you are Selling Short.  Once you have enough capital, "walk" the Chairman of the Board to assess liens on your competition and force them into bankruptcy.  A series of 300 cards determine how stock prices fluctuate.

A cute little game where you have large items you need to get home and a mutual friend Drew has a truck that can transport those things for you.  Competition revolves around getting the truck to go where you want it to go and arranging for there to be room in the truck for your things.

Aside from moving the truck around the board,  there are also cards you can play to repack the truck, drop things off the truck, give other players flat tires or bad backs or otherwise impede their progress.

The truck is metal and the things are differently shaped magnets. Very much like Tetris in a moving vehicle.

Online Play

     CCG Workshop (no longer available)

A card and board game where a wine novice can beat a connoisseur.
Worldwide Wines patented mode of play features "Region Cards", which provide the game's strategic element and "Money Cards", which educate players to the many aspects of wines of the world. 
Players must collect and BUY certain combinations of "Region Cards", which relate directly to the 106 wine regions on the game board map (Winning "R. Card" Combinations:3 stars/$24 Million each OR 4 wine bottles/$18 Million each OR 5 wine glasses/$14 Million each OR a "flush").  
Over 300 "Money Cards" present players with a wide range of challenging questions and interesting facts on the wine regions, their wine styles and production methods.  The "Money Cards" also give OR take away the cash needed to buy strategic "Region Cards".  WILD "Opportunity Cards" (included in the "Region Card" deck) give players the opportunity to buy owned "Region Cards" right out from under opponents.
All players have an equal chance of winning due to the "Region Card" strategy aspect.

boardgame: Power Power: The Werecat Card Battle
description:
A card game where you try to raise your catgirl (a girl with cat-like features, such as cat-ears and a tail) from a kitten to an adult before the other players do so with their own catgirl.

A version 2.00 was released with a new set of cards and new rules (not just 'errata' fixes or minor tweaks, but a reworking).

A fantasy collectible card game with many of the same themes as Magic: The Gathering. You have a bunch of monsters that you move along land that it set between the two players (with the monsters actually on the land, unlike Magic, but somewhat like the CCG 'Guardians').

Was fairly popular, with at about 4 expansions before a sequel was produced.

An unusual collectible card game that centers on cute characters as its selling point. Each player plays someone who has become class president of a girl’s high school classroom, and tries to raise a group to save the world from destruction (and battle against the other classes).

Based off the Vampire Savior arcade game. Each player takes a particular character, and constructs a deck with attacks usable by that character, and fight it out.

While there was a release of a "Vampire Savior 2", it really doesn't warrant a separate entry, as it is just the characters from the arcade game that the first series didn't cover.

One on one collectible card game with a strong emphasis on cute girls drawn by a variety of artists. The theme goes all over the place (fantasy, science fiction, etc). Was very popular, spawning a ton of expansions, multiple art books, a sequel game( Aquarian Age 2), drama CDs, anime, and misc. other merchandise.

boardgame: 女子高体育祭 (Girls High School Athletic Festival)
description: Try to overcome different difficult athletic events while trying to become the most successful at a high school athletics festival (at least, more successful than the other players). Use items to improve your odds of winning.

Collectible trading card game based off the Langrisser video game series. The main set is based off Langrissers 1-5, and has art by Satoshi Urushihara. There was one expansion set produced, based off Langrisser Millenium, but has a different artist than the main set.

The game has many similarities to Magic, but some major differences, such as no land cards. However, you have generals and monsters that you use to protect 'yourself' much the same way monsters protect 'yourself' in Magic.

Monster Jam Truck Game: In this fast-paced never-before-seen game, kids can re-create the rugged challenges and outrageous antics they see on Monster Jam® television shows. This is the first board game to feature the massively popular Monster Trucks. The game features Hot Wheels licenses, including He-Man and Gravedigger, the hottest Monster Truck around. Cards guide the players through the jumps and stunts of the game.

RPM is a word game played on a revolving board. The board is divided into 4 quarters. Each quarter has a top and bottom section. The object of the game is to form and capture words of 2 or more letters by placing letter tiles, one per quarter, as the board revolves. All players make their moves at the same time. The board makes exactly 5 revolutions before it is automatically stopped. This gives each player 20 opportunities to place his tiles. Colored tiles are used to capture completed words.

From the Box:

"Have you ever had a few minutes between games or between turns in a long game and needed something to do?  This is just the game for you.  Get your friends and play this simple, fast, real-time game of pattern recognition.  You can adjust the level of difficulty to suit the time available and skill of those involved.  A few of the hundreds of variants are supplied with the game.

Contents:
1 Playing mat with inscribed scoring and example patterns.
1 Set of the required dice [each player needs own set].
1 Instruction sheet. "

boardgame: Stock Market: The Wall Street Trading Game
description: The idea of the game is to trade stocks: buying, selling and selling short so profitably that one becomes the richest trader and the winner. A player is chosen to 'function' as the Stockbroker. The Market is opened with the turning up of the top Ticker Tape, then unlimited trading in lots of 100 shares may begin. News Cards affect stock prices up and down. A Record is kept of every trade and each change in price. A Market Closing Time is set, at which time all trading stops. Players and Broker settle up at the last price on the record. The game is one of fun, excitement, skill, and educational value.

"A very simple yet exciting family game for 2 OR 4 players Ages 6 and up"

Yes , very simple rules. Each player has 5 men of the same color. These men have to be moved to the other side of the board to win. Movement is by dice and only straight forward (no change of direction allowed).

When  players land in other player pieces that second piece goes back to the start : HOME YOU GO!!

The fun is when 4 people play, as "attackers" will come from all the 3 sides!!! as the board becomes more and more busy.

It has some simple strategy. Children and adults will like it.

boardgame: Hole in One Golf Card Game
description:
A simple card game designed to replicate a round of golf. Separate the cards into 6 decks. Correct order - Driver, Long Iron, Short Iron, Wedge, Putter.
1. Game follows the established rules of golf.
2. Driver must be used to tee off on par-4 and par-5 holes.
3. Short Iron must be used to tee off on par-3 holes.
4. Each card shows strokes and next club to be used.
5. All driver shots must be completed before Woods or Long Irons.
6. All Woods and Long Irons must be completed before Short Irons.
7. All short Iron shots must be completed before Putter.
8. All Wedge shots must be completed before Putter.
9. Fringe of green putts first followed by farthest distance from cup.
10. All Putters putt out before next opponent or opponents putt.
11. Replay nine holes for eighteen hole score.
12. One, two, three or four people may play.
13. All cards must be re-shuffled after each hole.
14. High score on each hole shuffles. Low score tees off first.

boardgame: Monster Maker Resurrection: The Monster Maker TCG
description:
Fantasy game with the Monster Maker characters. Vaguely similar to Magic, but only in the broadest sense.

Unlike the rest of the Mosnter Maker card games, this one is a collectible card game where players construct their own decks, instead of a fixed-deck set. Also, while the original artist does have cards he drew in this set, it is the first Monster Maker card game to use art from a variety or artists.

There are expansion sets produced for this game.

boardgame: 蓬萊カードゲーム：がんばれ体育祭 (Hourai Card Game: Ganbare Athletics Meet)
description:
A card game based off a high school athletics competition.

Cards with different values to the winner are used to represent different competitions, and you also have characters with different levels of athletic ability to use to try to win them. You can't reuse your athletes, so you'd like to use your best athlete for a high value competition, but then again, your opponents would want to do the same, and you may use your best athlete in hand for nothing because someone else has an even better one; whereas you could have used a lousy athlete in that case and while still would lose that competition, still have a very good athlete to use later.
Instead of a few adventurers entering a dungeon to fight monsters, in this game, a militia goes down into the dungeon. That militia is composed of the likes of pikeman and infantry.

A fantasy card game in which each player draws a character card and victory conditions card to start the game. An example of a victory condition would be "Gather 600 gold so you can buy medicine to cure your lover who's fallen ill."

there are many locations in the dungeon, and certain monsters can only be played in certain locations. Each monster card shows its name, strength, where it can appear, the treasure it carries, and the experience gained for killing the monster. There are of course items and weapons to help you.
In this game, each player uses his Spaceship and Super Weapon cards to attack the other players, and try to take over territory in space.

Contemporary war card game. There are six groups (mostly countries, but NATO is one of them) that fight, and each unit card is usable by only certain countries.

Air, naval, and ground attacks are all covered, and there are event cards for things like ammunition shortages as well.

In this game, you play detectives trying to do the best job solving crimes. Different crimes have different success level rankings in 3 different categories, with a value attached to solving the crime. You want to solve 1500 points worth of crimes before the other players.

Science fiction mecha fighting game. The object is to be the last player alive. There are weapon, defense, support, and special cards. You power up your robot with those cards and try to defeat everyone else's robots.

A card game set in recent/modern day Japan. Enlist the use of various school clubs (such as the Chemistry Club and Bunny-Girl Club) to help you in your quest to command the school via taking over the most territory in it.

Alien Highschool is a Japanese card game in which aliens have been infiltrating the Earth for more than 10 years.  Their target is a super-weapon that lies beneath a metropolitan high school for girls.  When the students learned of their plans, they band together to fend off the secret invasion.  Players assume the roles of these students and use their cards to foil the alien schemes.  When all incidents have been resolved, the player who foiled the most schemes is the winner.  One of the player, however, could be an alien in disguise and might betray the team at any time.

Tower Quest is a card game set in a fantasy world.  Players attempt to collect a set of cards to become the winner.

Each player is given a hand of 7 cards at the start, while all the other cards are mixed into a common deck.  One player starts the game by drawing a card from the deck and playing it.  Character cards (those with an icon) are placed face down, while other cards are played face up.  Going clockwise, the next player plays a card of higher value from his or her hand or passes.  The player who drew and played the initial card is the last to decide whether to play a card of higher value or not.  The player who played the highest value card in the pile gains the initial card into his or her collection; all other cards are discarded.  Players replenish their hands after the conclusion of this round and the next round begins with the next player.

Once a player has collected a pre-determined set of Character cards (based on their icons), he or she can call an end to the game.  The players tally their scores from the cards in their hand and collection, and add any bonuses (for ending the game, the collected Character cards, etc).  The player with the highest score is the winner.

A fantasy game with unique monsters as opposed to having each combat simply being a different strength rating die roll. Monsters each have their own set of attacks: A Cockatrice has "Stone x2" and "Claw x2" for its attack, and is immune to holy spells and vulnerable to fire; A Ghoul has "Fang x1" and "Poison x2" attacks, and is immune to stone, song, poison, and curse based attacks.

This game is monsters vs. monsters, neither player has characters that act as adventurers/heroes. There is the usual variety of spells and items and events to affect the outcomes of combat between the monsters.

A Fantasy card game, with art by the writer/artist of "Dragon Half". You play monsters, friends, and items. You defeat monsters to gain experience, and when you reach 4th level, you can then fight the final boss to win the game.

boardgame: レムリカシリーズⅢ：アームズコレクション (Lemlican Series III: Arms Collection)
description:
Final part of an ambitious 3-part attempt to make an RPG card game. This set provides many weapons, as well as various cards meant to make it also playable stand-alone. 
However, it was really meant to be used with the others in the 3-part set.

Expands

    レムリカシリーズⅠ：レムリカ銃士隊 (Lemlican Series Ⅰ: Lemlican Musketeers Card RPG)

boardgame: Gunbuster: トップをねらえ！ (Gunbuster: Aim for the Top!)
description:
Alternate name: "Gunbuster : Top wo nerae!"

In this game, you play female giant robot pilots defending the human race from aliens. However, the girls are still high-school-age students, and spend as much time teasing and tormenting each other as they do fighting. You can put a tack on the cockpit seat of another girl, write graffiti all over someone else's robot, and spread rumors of them being lesbian, among other things.You encourage your own character, and yes, you do fight the aliens.

Art is by Mika Akitaka, who is the artist behind "Galaxy Fraulien Yuna" and is the mech designer of "Martian Successor Nadesico"

boardgame: レムリカシリーズⅡ：レムリカモンスター (Lemlican Series Ⅱ: Lemlican Monsters)
description:
2nd of an ambitious 3-part attempt to make an RPG card game. This set provides the monsters for the game.

Also playable as a stand-alone game, but really meant to be used with the others in the 3-part set.

Expands

    レムリカシリーズⅠ：レムリカ銃士隊 (Lemlican Series Ⅰ: Lemlican Musketeers Card RPG)

boardgame: Wizardry Card Game Wiz Ball Expansion Kit
description:
This expansion requires the Wizardry Wiz Ball base game. This adds items and spells to the game, as well as more characters.

Expands:

    Wizardry Card Game Wiz Ball

boardgame: レムリカシリーズⅠ：レムリカ銃士隊 (Lemlican Series Ⅰ: Lemlican Musketeers Card RPG)
description:
An ambitious 3-part attempt to make an RPG card game. This is the base set, this set providing characters, items, and some events.

Also playable as a stand-alone game, but really meant to be used with the others in the 3-part set.

Expanded by

    レムリカシリーズⅡ：レムリカモンスター (Lemlican Series Ⅱ: Lemlican Monsters)
    レムリカシリーズⅢ：アームズコレクション (Lemlican Series III: Arms Collection)

boardgame: THEカラテマスター (The Karate Master)
description: A Karate fighting game, and one of the few card games from Sho-Kikaku that does not use any Monster Maker characters.

A fantasy card game about a groups/parties of characters that have been given a second chance at life. The players play good or evil angels, who grant parties of adventurers a second chance at life. You try to have them finish quests to support your cause.
artists: たかみよしひさ / Yoshihisa Takami

description:
A fantasy sports game, fairly close in rules to baseball. There are many minor rules differences, and characters can be injured as well. You have a team of characters that each have their own statistics. You play cards based on what you pitch you expect the other team to throw, or attempt to throw a type of pitch the other team isn't expecting.

There is an expansion that adds items and spells to the game.

Expanded by:

    Wizardry Card Game Wiz Ball Expansion Kit

Sequel to the original, this is a stand alone game. It retains the theme (with a fantasy setting) of curing various ills of patients. Problems such as "critical hit" (an arrow in the forehead) abound, and events such as an uncured patient turning to an Undead can occur.

Comical medical game. Slight fantasy feel, with catgirls and the like. Try to cure the illnesses of various people, with some occasionally less-than-efficient nurses (like the intern who completely slips when using her scalpel).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: It will have you rolling on the floor with laughter... If you can even get the words out. Play this Seriously Silly game to learn from others what an "inflatable chicken" is and what a "mentholated hairline" is for. The trick is getting your opponent to believe you. So what are "freeze-dried underwear" for and what kind of person owns "turbo sheep"?
"Black Widow" is a World War II air combat card wargame published in Japan. Its a direct combat game; each card is a plane rated in many categories.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Monster Maker: The Queen of Darkness
description:
An amateur but near-professional quality unlicensed expansion for Monster Maker. Has additional cards, treasures, and events for Monster Maker. Requires the original Monster Maker game.

Expands:

    Monster Maker

The Monster Maker characters in a science-fiction theme. The game remains similar to the later games in the Monster Maker series, with player, monster, and treasure cards (but of course, no corridor cards).

Amateur and very unofficial/unlicensed game based off Monster Maker. The box calls it "A Sexual Combat of Beautiful Gals in Monster Maker world". Sexual items are used to "arouse" the characters. Sorry, only the cover will be scanned, this is a family webpage.

Similar to the original Gal Master 1, this is a stand alone game. This adds a few new card types, such as one that requires a player to return all of their defeated monsters (and therefore go back to zero points), and ones that double or reverse the value of monsters.

Detailed World War 2 card game that covers the ground war. Very ambitous, it covers many countries and their infantry, artillery, and tanks.

An optional expansion named Tank Buster was released, which adds terrain and event cards.

Expanded by:

    Tank Buster

While it says it can be played as a stand-alone game, it is meant to be played as an expansion to Tank Hunter, and adds terrain and events to the units from the main game.

Expands:

    Tank Hunter

In this game, each player starts with a set of 12 hero cards, numbered 1-12. The main deck of monsters is played one at a time, and each monster has a value (a few are even negative). Players then each play a hero card, and the high card kills the monster, and the player gets points equal to the value of the monster. If there is a tie, the highest non-tied character wins, so the winner when heroes rated 9,9,5,and 4 are played is the player who played the 5). At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

Players play the role of horror hunters, attempting to defeat monsters both magical (like vampires) and mundane (like psycho killers). The game has a light-hearted feel, with (unlicensed) use of many horror characters such as Jason, Freddy, and the girl from "The Exorcist."

Players are rated with attack strength and spiritual strength values. Monsters have strength ratings, terror points, and value ratings for when they're defeated. Events can lower your spiritual strength, and various insanities can afflict your character. Visits to a psychologist can restore your spiritual strength.

A World War 2 card game with mechanics similar to the same company's Monster Maker game. You play distance cards to represent the German's travel towards Moscow. There are enemy troops to interfere with your movement, and time cards simulate the passage of time (Winter saves Moscow!).

Includes rules for solo play, or works as a 2-6 player game as well.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Side-story to the main Monster Maker series, this is the 2nd in a series of card games featuring the Monster Maker characters in a still-fantasy high school setting.

In this game, there are various events at the school festival worth points to the player who plays the highest rating (1-10); if there is a tie, the highest non-tied ranking wins the event (so the winner when scores of 9,9,5,and 4 are played is the player who played the 5). Some events are unpopular and worth negative points that you don't want to win.

Alternate title: Monster Maker Shuugaku Ryoukou

Side-story to the main Monster Maker series, this is the 3rd in a series of card games featuring the Monster Maker characters in a still-fantasy high school setting.

In this game, everyone goes on school vacations. You need to find someone to go together with; certain characters get along better with each other. You need to find an airplane that goes to the destination card you want to go to. Going on vacations to exotic places are worth the most points; vacations to the Dark Land are negative points.

Alternate name: Monster Maker Gakuen

Side-story to the main Monster Maker series, this is the 1st in a series of card games featuring the Monster Maker characters in a still-fantasy high school setting.

In this one, try to match up characters to make them fall in love, but there are such things to worry about like having an ex-girlfriend Succubus.

If you like "Risk" and "Axis & Alies", you'll love this game. It's all the fun with half the learning. The year is 1520, a time of political and military upheaval as great empires juggled for world power. Meet face to face with great empires such as Ottoman, Iberian, Spanish, Mughal, Safid, Russian, Quing, Japanese, French, English, and more. Will you be the next great world leader? Play this classic strategy game and find out?

For details see http://ajaster.com/biglittlegames/europe1520.html

Alternate name: Star Blazers Be forever Game

Based off the Space Battleship Yamato anime (known as Star blazers in the U.S.), the game comes in a very oversized box. It has a large board with a lot of different sections on it, with different gameplay on different parts of the board to simulate the epic story of the show.

boardgame: Cat's Eye: The Last Big Game
description:
Based off the "Cat's Eye" anime show from the early 80s, about a trio of women thieves that wear leotard outfits and steal treasures.

Oversize game, with a very big multi-part board, along with many components (event cards, chips, information cards, roulette, playing pieces, and such).

Part of Bandai's "Game for Adult ~IF" series.

Game for Adult meaning aimed at older players, not anything with objectionable content.

Complexity 2 on Bandai's 1-7 scale (1=easiest)

"~If" just refers to the their gimmick of setting up each game in the series as a "What if... (insert game scenario)"

This particular game is based on the anime of the same name, and stars the character Captain Harlock. Uses large counters and area movement without multiple boards.

Pargolf does an amazing job of simulating the actual game of golf. The game is played over a challenging 6970 yard par 72 test of skill judgement and luck. It requires all the shots you have in the bag.

5 Card Nancy has never been published, so all existing sets have been constructed by hand.
 The game is essentially an exercise in collaborative storytelling, played with a deck of cards. Each card in the deck features a single, unique panel taken from Ernie Bushmiller's classic newspaper comic-strip "Nancy." The strip is still in syndication today (and currently drawn by Olivia Jaimes), but McCloud insists that only panels from Bushmiller's peak years on the strip -- 1946 and later -- are suitable for use in a 5 Card Nancy deck.
 Game play is very simple: an initial card is selected randomly from the draw pile and placed face up on the table. This card will serve as the first panel in the collaborative comic strip that will emerge as a result of gameplay. On her turn, a player will then select a single card from her hand, and place that card face up and to the right of the emerging comic strip. A judge or judges (usually the other players) then vote on the appropriateness of that particular panel to the unfolding narrative. If majority opinion favors the proposed panel, it becomes a part of the strip, otherwise it returns to the hand of the player who proposed it.

5 Card Nancy is a card game invented by cartoonist Scott McCloud, author of "Understanding Comics" and "Reinventing Comics," and creator of the "Zot!" series of comic books (among others). McCloud describes the game as a "Dada" game, by which he means a game in the spirit of the Dada movement.

The players take turns playing as Body defenses/medication/etc. (one per round), while all the other players play as external agents trying to take over the control of the body.  The battle takes place using a schema of the human body, and the winner is the player, when playing as defense, who can eliminate all the aggressors.
It's a Brazilian boardgame made by one of the best game creator's of this country.

Golfer's Dominoes is a companion set to Dr. Miller's Golf Ace, with similar packaging and somewhat jocular, quirky rules, but there the similarity ends. Dominoes is, mechanically, a much simpler game, based on playing the most advantageous domino depicting one of seven possible golfing situations, from your hand of 4. The 28 dominoes are made of stiffened card and bear simple cartoon-style drawings of a golf ball lying on the fairway, beside a bunker, in a bunker, beside water, in water, beside the hole and in the hole. The player managing to place legitimately an "in the hole" domino with no hazard domino showing on any of the four ends of the pattern wins the hole.  There is no counting of "strokes". The victor is simply the one who wins most holes.

Similar to the word game ghosts. Players alternate playing letter cards onto a string of cards that eventually forms a word - and when it does the person placed the last letter wins some chips from the pot - and the person who couldn't continue antes some chips. Cards can be played to front or end of the word, and there are some special cards, like wilds, and "inserts".

A player draws a card that poses a question, and each  player predicts how others will answer. Players wager an amount on their prediction and then the question is answered, presumably honestly. The flavor hook to this game is that all players are immensely wealthy, and all the questions are about what they are going to do now that they are loaded.

A speed based game with 8 different card types. Players maintain a hand of 5 cards. Each time a card is played on the single play pile, it opens up the possibility for certain other cards to be played by any player. What cards you can play will depend on whether or not you have possession and what card preceded it. For example, a block can be played after a shot, but not after a 3 point shot, and after playing it the player would have possession, opening up the opportunity for a shot from them.

This is billed as "the ultimate tic-tac-toe" and has some resemblance to nine men's morris. The array is 4x4, and players alternate placing one of their 6 pieces on, placing one of the two wild pieces on, or moving/jumping with one of their pieces or the wild pieces. The goal is to get 4 in a row, make a square (2x2), or occupy all 4 corners. There are a number of anti-stalling rules and stakes raising, as in backgammon to round out the game.

1993 Mensa Select

With Cargo you are a forwarder and international trader. You buy goods an world markets, and , as a forwarder, you organize suitable means of transport, work out the routing, take over the forwarding jobs such as filling out the freight documents, customs clearing and shipping advices and in this way ensure streamlined progress from  dispatcher to consignee. You will also have to struggle with the problems involved in forwarding but with good calculations, good counting and also a little luck you'll become a forwarder and businessman of worldwide experience...

boardgame: And Now For Something Completely Different... The Board Game
description:
The game plays like Snake & Ladders on Spam.

Players line their markers on the “It’s” starting space, roll the die, move forward and try to reach the last space, an illustration of the Python foot. Should a marker land on certain spaces (rose stems and very long, Terry Gilliam-cartoon-like, winding arms), it goes backwards or forwards a certain number of spaces. A pretty simple, straightforward fare up until now, right? Right!
Now the Pythonian fun begins... Several spaces along the track are special and come in three different varieties: Task, Trivia and Character Type.

Task spaces require the active player to draw a Task card and perform the task described on it (e.g. Explain your theory on spontaneous combustion in which must be included goats, a kettle, Venus, a jelly babies and a sock with a hole in it). The other players then vote on the active player’s performance and, should the vote be positive, he gets an Award card (more on them later).

Trivia spaces require another player other than the active player do draw a Trivia card and read the Python-related question on it. If the answer is correct, another Award card is given.

Character types spaces have, well... character types on them (e.g. Canadian lumberjack, Spanish waiter, etc.) which the active player must take on as a persona until his next turn. If the other players decide he didn’t put in enough of an effort, they can just move his marker back to the starting space...

Finally, the Award cards. Apart from some flavor, most of these cards serve no other purpose other than being able to display your proud array of awards. The ones that do have game text on them require the player to perform some sort of task until the end of the game to keep the award.

With a hand of 8 cards, players must either play a lower card than one on display, taking chips from the kitty, a higher card, (paying chips into the kitty), or multiple cards which match the number on display. When one player plays all cards, the other players must pay that player chips equal to the remaining value of cards in their hand.

Your creations slowly come to life in the heart of the laboratory.

Have you been smart.

LAB is a miniatures game where you construct electrifying machines to feed your Kryo-chamber with resources. Then your dreadful creations emerge from the inner depths of your machine.

Every monster has  unique features and the challenge is for you to construct a laboratory capable of creating the monsters you want in your horde. After that, you only have to make them obey...

The key to victory is to construct a laboratory optimal to the strategy you have chosen, and use it tactically while defending it from the opponent monsters and ordering your monsters to raze his laboratory.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion #1: England & Ireland
description:
Age of Steam expansion set with a double sided mapboard with England/Wales on one side and Ireland on the reverse.

The English/Wales map is designed for 5/6 players.

The Irish Map is designed for 3/4 players and has new rules to cover the special development of the Irish railway system.

Wild is a cribbage additive... it is a deck of 31 playing cards that are set beside the crib board. When a player counts a multiple of 5, that player draws a Wild card and follows the instructions on it.

Promotional family game, published by Mercedes Benz & Ravensburger.

Theme
A road trip with grandma, grandpa, mum, dad and three children, who all have to be put into the car so that you can be the first to reach the holiday destination.

Goal
Be the first to reach the finish line by successfully completing activities and being lucky in a roll-and-move part.

Game play
The game is divided into two phases:

In phase 1, players try to fill their car (= their player board) as fast as possible. They do so by completing tasks from the activity cards. 
The first area of the board shows a track consisting of spaces in six different colours. There is one pawn for all the players. Players take turns rolling the die and move the pawn accordingly. Depending on the colour of the space the pawn lands on, the active player has to 

     mime or draw one of two words from the card and the other players guess what it is, 
     read a story from the card and everyone guesses if it is true or false, 
     guess if the other players will raise an even or an odd number of fingers, 
     write down what they associate with a word, and the other players have to guess. 


Players who successfully complete a task and players who guess correctly get to spin a spinner showing the different family members. If they haven't already got this family member, they take a matching chip and put it on their player board. Otherwise they have to gift it to someone else.

If the pawn lands on a light or dark pink space, players play "The magical 7" (trying to come as close as possible to 7 with two die rolls - the winner spins the spinner) or "Spinner poker" (betting one of your chips to get to spin the spinner twice - if the spinner points towards the family member you were betting on, you win that chip, otherwise you lose the one you bet).

Phase 2: Once a player has filled his/her car, he/she proceeds to the second part of the board. Here, each player has a car pawn. Players try to reach the finish line as fast as possible. Obstacle tokens and event spaces make you lose or win family members. If a player lands on a space that is already occupied, he/she can steal a family member to fill an empty spot in their own car.

The first player to reach the finish line wins the game.

Lakeedah is an exciting strategy game that's fast to learn and helps build critical thinking and planning skills while having fun. Both children and adults enjoy Lakeedah's varieties of play, which includes two-player, four-player, and team play variations that span the range from very strategic to fast and tactical.

Lakeedah is designed as an "Open Source" game, that is, players have the ability to create new game variations and submit them to this website and for possible inclusion in the Official Rulebook.

www.lakeedah.com (no longer available)

(from the box)

A 3-Dimensional fearful fun game - but watch out!  The ghosts are out to get you.

Turn the wheels and run the gauntlet of ghastly ghouls!  Get caught in the spookey spider's web!  Sit with skeletons in the haunted waiting room!


Ghost Train is a British game based on those haunted amusement park rides.

The gameboard simulates the inside of a 'Ghost Train' complete with lurking ghosts, bats, skeletons and spiders, etc.

The mechanics (spinning tracks which change direction of movement) are the same as those in the Walt Disney Haunted Mansion Ride Game.

The game simulates many of the 'quirks' of such rides including sudden and jolting changes of direction, dead stops, being 'stuck' at one of the exhibits, and mechanical moving spooks.

Game Play:

Players enter the Ghost Train form the outside in little buggies and navigate the ride while being subjected to detours, directional changes, dead ends, delays and all sorts of creepy monsters.

Players can occasionally spin the turntables to either change a buggies direction (inside the circle) or have an attacked spook knock over an opponents buggy back (outside track) sending it to the 'waiting room'.

There is also a giant swinging spider guarding the exit!
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
This is a simple family boardgame. Each player operates a delivery company which deliver goods for customers from one city to another via airplanes. Players play card from their hands to finish the delivery job. Amount of money obtained is also written on the cards.

On the map, each city can only be occupied by a player. So hand management is important in planning ones' own delivery routes.

If you have to smile when you think back to the cheesy special effects of your childhood, you’ll love this nod to the classic “supermarionation” series, Thunderbirds. More than a nod to nostalgia, this game features completing exciting missions to win. The board features Tracy Island in the center (which is home base) and five zones: Space, Air, Oil Rig, Underground and City. Draw Mission cards to see where you’ll go, who will lead, what vehicle you’ll use, what you will accomplish and how many points your mission is worth. Complete the mission and return to Tracy Island safely and you'll get the points. At the end of the game, add up your points to see who wins!

Contents: 
66 Thunderbird Cards 
25 Mission Cards
24 Equipment Cards
17 Thunderbird 5 Cards
6 Pawns
1 die.

For 2 to 6 players.

The object of the game is to be the first player to get rid of all of their waste without going broke.  The theme is about recycling products, and appropriately, the game components are all recycled materials: milk caps for movers, various beads and plastic chips for the waste products, etc.

L'Encanteur - the French fish auctioneer
The bidding's master of ceremony - l'encanteur - used to sing prices.
The song-bidding system is a two-phase process : first, the price decreasing phase and then the price increasing phase.
Each player impersonates a restaurant owner.

Goal : Be the first to serve your secret fish-combination after having made your shopping at the song-bidding.
Game turns are played by pairs : morning is when you go to the song-bidding and buy your different species of fish or seafood, afternoon is when you try to serve the best your clients.

Number of players : 3 or 4
Ages 8 and up
Average length : 15-20 mn.
Number of cards : 110 cards (rulebook in French, English, Dutch, German)

Each player has four merchant ships which they are trying to race across their own "side" of the board to the home port. They also have a submarine (which can attack the merchants) and a destroyer (which can counter-attack the sub).

The game is basically like "Battleships" with movement. The cool part of the game is the board.

The board stands up vertically and each player plays his own side of the board. The ships stay in place as their bases are short pegs that fit pretty tightly in holes on the board. The board is made of a sandwich of two thick card boards with a thin sheet of rubbery plastic in between, so you can't see which holes your opponents ships are in. But you can push your "torpedo" and "depth charge" through the hole, stretching out the rubber and popping your opponents ship off the board. Coolest physical game mechanic ever! No, wait, it gets better! At the bottom of the board is a periscope. Yes, it really has mirrors. You slide this left and right to any column of the board, and then peek through the eyepiece to sight the enemy. You get a view of one column of the board and a peripheral view of the columns each side. If you get a sighting of an opponents ship on the central column you can try and estimate the range and torpedo it. The enemy destroyer then gets a shot at counterattacking your sub. The view through the periscope looks just like looking out from a sub and seeing ships peeping out of the ocean (nicely painted board) along the line of sight. Absolutely the coolest physical game mechanic ever!!!

Board: 13 x 13 square grid

Pieces each side: 4 Merchant ships, 1 Sub, 1 Destroyer, 1 Torpedo, 1 Depth Charge

User summary:

The playing field is put into a plastic frame and placed on edge. Each player equips the field on their side with ships, which are inserted into the holes in the playing field. An opaque plastic sheet between the layers of the playing field makes it impossible to see the opposing ship positions. At the bottom of the playing field a "periscope" is hooked, with which one can spy out the opponent's side of the playing field under limited conditions. The goal is to remove the enemy ships from their position with the help of torpedoes and depth charges. These are inserted into a hole on your own side and push out the enemy ship on the opposite side.

The winner is the player who is the last to have their own ships left in the playing field.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Commander is the game of airplane pilots. Each player must collect a certain number of flight Km, making his own career by piloting bigger and bigger airplanes, and choosing starting and ending airports. This is easily made by rolling dices to travel around the world and rolling other dices to be able to take off and land on each airport.

A furious scramble to circle the numbers from 60 down to 1 on a pad of paper. The catch? Only 1 pencil! While one player searches for numbers, the next player rolls a die continually. As soon as he rolls Quick Count! he grabs the pencil and begins searching for his numbers as the next person rolls the die.

In this funny and easy old game the players act as photo reporters. Their objective is to get the highest number of photos of some important people and then sell them, before those people travel away, making the news obsolete. To do this players roll the dice and walk in a maze like area of streets. Some major streets head towards particular places (Park, Theater, Industry, Airport, Zoo). Players roll dice and the roll of those dice decides which v.i.p. moves and where each goes.

Passtimes: The Game of History challenges players to answer questions about events in our past.  The game is played among groups or individuals, who are given pairs of tokens to move on the board.

When a player takes his or her turn, a card is drawn for them.  The player must answer a set of questions printed on that card.  If they answer a question correctly, they roll a die and move one of their tokens by the die's result.  An incorrect answer ends his or her turn and starts the next player's.  If a Time Warp card was drawn, the player switches one of his or her token with the token closes to the ending square.

The first player to move both of his or her tokens to the ending square wins and ends the game.

From the Rules Introduction:

Super Nova is a game of intergalactic expansion and conquest. Players start from their home worlds, discover new planets, and compete for control of other planets. Power is wielded militarily, diplomatically, and economically, with an occasional natural disaster thrown in. To win, you must create the empire that supports the most population.

Super Nova was originally published in a collectible format, but is now published as a 165-card complete game.

Expanded by:

    Super Nova: Event Horizon

Publisher's Description:

Acquire.  Liquidate.  Control.
Join the ranks of the global financial elite in Merge War.  Take control of an international corporation, acquire the building blocks of world monopolies, and mercilessly steal assets to thwart your rivals!  The first CEO to hit one trillion dollars wins, and has the world economy in his iron grip.  In this game for 2-4 players, do you have what it takes to reach the top?

Contents:
46 game cards
2 reference cards
2 rules cards
All cards are black and white, business card size, and packaged in a small zip lock bag.

A band of 12 poachers is sought out in Casablanca. Before they can be arrested, they succeed in escaping to the Rif and Atlas mountains. From there, their tracks lead to the south and the east of Africa.

The players in this game represent game wardens who try to follow the tracks of the poachers through Africa to their hiding place in order to arrest them, meeting many exotic animals along the way.

During the game, the players move their pawns on the spaces marked 1 to 36 and pick up the small tracking tiles, which show them, where the tracks of the poachers lead. Each player on his destination sheet draws in the tracks that he has uncovered. With each game, there is only one set of continuous tracks from the space marked 1 up to one of the hiding places (spaces A through K).

The goal of the game is to follow this continuous set of tracks and discover which hiding place the poachers are using. A player that knows or believes he knows the correct hiding place can arrest poachers from that space. The winner is the player who arrests the most poachers from the correct hiding place.

Go Bark™ is the matching card game that has it all: 4-color pictures of dog breeds, special strategy and lots of barking out loud. Use cards for traditional "Go Fish" play or the more advanced "Go Bark™" play. Special "Bark Cards™" will get you woofing and yapping to throw players off your scent. 54 premium playing cards plus instructions included. 48 Dog breed cards, 6 Bark Cards™ plus instruction card included. Teachers love Go Bark for educational play!
www.gobark.com

Hexostrategy games are an opportunity to play military and historical campaigns.  
It is an operational level game.
Players alternate their moves and resolve any combat as a result.

Hexostrategy: Battle of Lake Peipus – 1242 is dedicated to the events leading to the Battle of Lake Peipus. One player commands Hermann of Dorpat and the German Knights and the other the Novgorod Russian Troops led by Alexander Nevski using 1/72 miniatures on a folded map board.

description: Norseman is a game of the latter half of the Viking period. The game is played in a series of turns, each of which is composed of phases, each of which may contain one or more sequenced segments. Raid, trade, incursion, levy, and outright warfare are all covered.
